text: The laparoscopic transanal TME technique is demonstrated for excising Denon-Villiers fascia in a mid-rectal tumor. The surgeon performs medial to lateral dissection using hook diathermy, carefully identifying and safeguarding the ureter. The superior hypogastric nerves are preserved, and hi-tie ligation is performed on the inferior mesenteric artery (IMA). The inferior mesenteric vein (IMV) is cut at the inferior border of the pancreas, while the left testicular vessels are preserved. The colon is mobilized laterally towards the spleen.

The surgeon begins the total mesorectal excision (TME) dissection posteriorly towards the pelvic floor, marking the peritoneum near the peritoneal reflection to leave a small cuff attached to the mesorectum. This cuff aids in applying traction to open the mesorectal planes. The dissection progresses from front to sides to uniformly mobilize the rectum within the TME plane. Cutting current is used to minimize lateral coagulation and protect the nearby right and left inferior hypogastric nerves.
The surgeon carefully preserves the left inferior hypogastric plexus during the mobilization of the left side of the total mesorectal excision (TME).

The surgeon continues anterior dissection, revealing Denonvilliers fascia behind the prostate. Dissection proceeds in front of the fascia due to the presence of an anterior rectal tumor, utilizing the transanal TME technique.
The surgeon begins transanal dissection, applying a purse string suture to the rectal lumen. The dissection proceeds posteriorly in the TME plane, which is visually identifiable and easier to access.

The surgeon begins dissection in the TME plane behind the lower border of the prostate, which is covered by Denonvilliers fascia. The anterior visceral mesorectal fascia is difficult to identify in this TA-TME approach.

The surgeon receives guidance from the abdominal team to identify the Denonvilliers fascia from the prostatic capsule after abdominal dissection. The anterior and posterior layers of Denonvilliers fascia are demonstrated, and a precise cut is made in the anterior layer to excise the fascia with the specimen in cases of anterior rectal tumors.

The video shows a muscularis propria invading adenocarcinoma with two layers of Denon-Villiers fascia visible, while the fascia disappears laterally. The final histology reveals mucinous adenocarcinoma with clear circumferential and distal margins, measuring thirty-five millimeters. The TME is graded as three, indicating good quality.
